claim_statement: The paper claims domain-level lifted AgentSpeak(L) library synthesis for positive conjunctive achievement-goal domains whose reusable strategy is expressible as a goal-conditioned, feature-definable, terminating modular policy sketch with bounded sketch width and whose selected features can be safely bound to declared PDDL predicates, primitive actions, predicate subgoals, read-only goal descriptors, or read-only agenda gates.

out_of_core: arbitrary path-finding domains without accepted distance or ranking features

domain_families.name: Goal-independent or weakly ordered transport/control domains
domain_families.paper_role: Control and baseline candidate family, not the core novelty.
domain_families.implementation_status: Not claimed as strict paper evidence until the ASL library is generated and validated under the same no-runtime-planner protocol.
domain_families.why_it_matters: These domains show whether the ASL compiler can cover MOOSE-friendly and PG3-friendly generalized-planning cases without overclaiming goal dependency.

domain_families.name: Bounded-width sketchable subgoal-structure domains
domain_families.paper_role: Main theoretical superclass for our representation and feature-binding contract.
domain_families.implementation_status: Current strict evidence consumes learner-sketches Blocksworld blocks-on evidence; broader family members are classified as next evaluation targets unless separately validated.

domain_families.name: Goal-dependent but feature-definable construction domains
domain_families.paper_role: Core positive claim and primary achievement-goal benchmark family.
domain_families.implementation_status: Strict paper-profile Blocksworld rows solve first20, satisfiable-large, and satisfiable-mixed-large with selected external sketch evidence and no runtime full-trace planner.

domain_families.name: Ordered-resource and dead-end-avoidance domains
domain_families.paper_role: Mechanism stress family for Layer C and counterexample refinement.
domain_families.implementation_status: Current final package uses Labworkflow as a controlled stress domain. Spanner and Childsnack are preferred next standard benchmark targets.

domain_families.name: Route, transitive-closure, and resource-delivery boundary domains
domain_families.paper_role: Boundary and limitation family unless a principled lifted distance/ranking binding is selected and validated.
domain_families.implementation_status: Transport and Marsrover are reported only as initial-state or trace-evidence boundary rows; arbitrary reachable mid-state logistics policies are not part of the main claim.

domain_families.name: Numeric, object-specific, and unsupported temporal fragments
domain_examples: barman with unbound resource policy features
domain_families.paper_role: Explicit exclusion for the current achievement-goal paper.
domain_families.implementation_status: Rejected or deferred unless the missing semantics are implemented and validated.

Chen2026Moose.title: Satisficing and Optimal Generalised Planning via Goal Regression
Chen2026Moose.role: Goal independence taxonomy and MOOSE baseline boundary.

Drexler2022LearningSketches.title: Learning Sketches for Decomposing Planning Problems into Subproblems of Bounded Width
Drexler2022LearningSketches.role: Automatic sketch learning, answer-set formulation, and bounded sketch width.

Drexler2024SubgoalSketches.title: Expressing and Exploiting Subgoal Structure in Classical Planning Using Sketches
Drexler2024SubgoalSketches.role: Sketches as explicit subgoal structure and bounded-width decomposition.

Bonet2024GeneralPoliciesWidth.title: General Policies, Subgoal Structure, and Planning Width
Bonet2024GeneralPoliciesWidth.role: Planning-width and subgoal-structure theory behind the supported class.

Bonet2024PolicyReuse.title: On Policy Reuse: An Expressive Language for Representing and Executing General Policies that Call Other Policies
Bonet2024PolicyReuse.role: Modular policy-call representation closest to ASL modules.

Frances2021LearningGeneralPolicies.title: Learning General Planning Policies from Small Examples Without Supervision
Frances2021LearningGeneralPolicies.role: Feature-definable general policies and full Blocksworld support via well-placed features.

Yang2022PG3.title: PG3: Policy-Guided Planning for Generalized Policy Generation
Yang2022PG3.role: Lifted decision-list policy baseline and goal-conditioned policy-search comparison.

SegoviaAguas2022BFGPpp.title: Representation and Synthesis of C++ Programs for Generalized Planning
SegoviaAguas2022BFGPpp.role: Program-synthesis alternative for generalized plans; useful baseline but not the ASL target representation.
